description: Function-based access control with collection-level, field-level, and document-level permissions supporting boolean and WHERE clause results

actors.name: Authenticated User
actors.description: Any user authenticated via JWT, API key, or custom strategy

actors.name: Anonymous User
actors.description: Unauthenticated visitor — access functions receive null user

actors.name: Access Engine
actors.description: Evaluates access functions and merges results with database queries

where_clause_merging.description: When access returns a Where clause, it is AND-merged with the user query

override_access.description: Bypasses all access checks — used by admin operations, scheduled tasks, webhooks

disable_errors.description: Returns empty results instead of throwing Forbidden on access denial

access_granted_boolean.result: Operation proceeds with no additional query constraints

given: access function returns a Where clause object
then: Where clause merged with user query via combineQueries()
database query only returns documents matching both user query AND access filter
access_granted_where.result: Operation proceeds with row-level filtering applied

access_denied_silent.result: Empty result set returned (no error thrown)

given: field-level access function returns false for a specific field
then: field excluded from response (read access denied)
field value ignored in input (create/update access denied)
field_access_denied.result: Field silently excluded — no error, field just absent from response

given: user requests permission check via /access/:id endpoint
doc_access_check.result: Complete permission object returned with boolean flags for each operation and each field

given: user attempts to access admin panel for a collection
admin_panel_access.result: User granted or denied access to this collection in the admin UI

errors.message: You are not allowed to perform this action
You must be logged in to perform this action

events.name: access.denied
events.description: Emitted when access is denied — useful for audit logging

related.reason: Access control depends on authenticated user identity from auth system
Access functions are configured per collection
Globals have their own access control configuration

goals.description: Function-based access control with collection-level, field-level, and document-level permissions supporting boolean and WHERE clause results

tradeoffs.reason: access control must enforce least-privilege principle

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details

access_patterns.name: Public read, auth write
access_patterns.description: read returns true, create/update/delete return Boolean(req.user)

access_patterns.name: Owner-only
access_patterns.description: read/update/delete return Where clause filtering by userId field

access_patterns.name: Role-based
access_patterns.description: Access function checks req.user.role against allowed roles

access_patterns.name: Field-level redaction
access_patterns.description: Sensitive fields have read access returning false for non-admin users
